articles.title: US: Energy shock complicates Fed path – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Bernd Weidensteiner notes that U.S. inflation jumped to 3.3% in March, driven mainly by higher gasoline prices linked to the war in Iran, while core inflation remains moderate.

articles.title: WH Sr. Adviser Hassett: We expect a rapid reduction in energy prices once the Strait of Hormuz opens
articles.expand: Kevin Hassett, Director of the National Economic Council, told FOX Business on Friday that the Federal Reserve (Fed) outlook for having room to cut rates will be very solid. He also claimed the Hormuz Strait can be reopened within two months, saying they have backup plans to reopen it.

articles.title: Gold holds steady as US CPI rises in March, Fed outlook caps upside
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) holds steady on Friday but lacks strong upside momentum as markets continue to monitor the evolving situation in the Middle East, while traders digest the latest US inflation data.

articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: GBP rises on Russia-Ukraine peace optimism
articles.expand: The Pound Sterling (GBP) trades higher against its major currency peers, except other European currencies, edging up to near 1.3444 against the US Dollar (USD) during the European trading session on Friday.

articles.title: NZD: Hawkish pricing seen as demanding – OCBC
articles.expand: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong note New Zealand Dollar (NZD) has rallied on hawkish Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) rhetoric and easing Oil risks, but warns markets may be overpricing tightening.

articles.title: GBP/JPY Price Forecast: Bulls eye breakout above 214.00-215.00 resistance
articles.expand: GBP/JPY edges higher on Friday, extending gains for a fifth straight day as the Japanese Yen (JPY) remains on the defensive against most of the major peers. Elevated Oil prices continue to weigh on the Yen, given Japan’s status as a major net importer.

articles.title: Breaking News: US inflation surges to 2-year high of 3.3%, Dollar Index (DXY) slips as US-Iran talks in focus
articles.expand: US inflation hits a 2-year high of 3.3% due to a massive energy "war premium" from geopolitical conflict. Core CPI remains contained at 2.6%, creating a "sticky" situation for the Fed. Markets watch US-Iran talks, which are steering the US Dollar Index (DXY).

articles.title: US CPI at 3.3% Misses Expectations, Second-Round Effects Still Limited
articles.expand: US inflation surged in March, with CPI rising 0.9% mom and lifting the annual rate from 2.4% yoy to 3.3% yoy. While the jump was significant, both figures came in slightly below expectations of 1.0% mom and 3.4% yoy respectively, suggesting the energy-driven spike was largely anticipated. The surge was overwhelmingly driven by energy costs. [&#8230...

articles.title: Breaking: US CPI inflation jumps to 3.3% in March, as forecast
articles.expand: Annual inflation in the United States (US), as measured by the change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI), climbed to 3.3% in March, the US Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reported on Friday. This print followed 2.4% in February and came in line with market expectations.

articles.title: NBP expands gold reserves and moves closer to the global Top 10
articles.expand: The NBP increased its gold reserves in March 2026 by about 13 tons, taking advantage of a sharp price correction. Total holdings rose to around 580 tons. Despite a lower valuation due to falling prices, the central bank continues its strategy of accumulation and is now just over 30 tons away from entering the global top 10 in gold reserves.

articles.expand: The markets are treading water as the North American session gets underway, with little conviction across the major asset classes ahead of key economic releases. articles.title: EUR: Tightening expectations support Euro – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Francesco Pesole highlights that markets now price only 6bp for a European Central Bank (ECB) hike on 30 April, while around 55bp remains priced by year-end.

articles.title: India: Gradual energy pass-through keeps RBI patient – DBS
articles.expand: DBS Group Research projects India’s March Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation to edge up to 3.45% year-on-year from 3.2%, reflecting higher cooking gas, energy and input costs, while retail fuel and food stay benign. Precious metals’ correction tempers price pressures.

articles.title: USD/CAD Forecast: Bears turn cautious ahead of US-Iran talks, US CPI, Canadian jobs data
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair gains some positive traction on Friday, snapping a four-day losing streak to the 1.3800 neighborhood or over a two-week low set the previous day. Doubts over the durability of the US-Iran ceasefire support the safe-haven US Dollar (USD) and act as a tailwind for spot prices.

articles.expand: With the two-week US-Iran ceasefire under strain, crude oil sits at a technical crossroads as traders are holding out for further developments to gauge if a brief dip or a longer-term pullback is due.

articles.title: ADB: Azerbaijan's economy will grow by an average of 1.9% in 2026-2027
articles.expand: The Asian Development Bank (ADB) expects Azerbaijan's GDP to increase by 2% in 2026 and by 1.8% in 2027, according to the ADB's Asian Development Outlook for April, Report informs.

The US dollar partially recouped its losses from the previous session, as market sentiment remains cautious amid a fragile truce between the US and Iran. articles.title: The Petrodollar trade is over, Dollar tumbles – EUR/USD, AUD/USD & Dollar Index (DXY) overview
articles.expand: US Dollar, AUD/USD and EUR/USD Analysis: The Greenback’s two-month Petrodollar rally takes a massive hit as an unexpected US-Iran ceasefire agreement shatters war-driven demand. articles.title: EURUSD consolidates near highs and holding above a cluster of technical levels
articles.expand: The EURUSD surged higher following the cease-fire announcement after the close yesterday, but the bullish move was already taking shape earlier in the session. articles.title: EUR/USD: Testeando una Resistencia Sólida tras un Repunte Notable
articles.expand: El EUR/USD recuperó impulso tras la relajación de las tensiones geopolíticas, aunque el mercado sigue pendiente de las actas de la Fed y de los próximos datos clave de inflación en Estados Unidos.

articles.title: The USDCAD is lower but it is seeing more of a bounce. What would worry the seller?
articles.expand: The USDCAD moved lower as cease-fire optimism out of the Middle East helped shift the broader market tone. The news weighed on the USD, while stocks pushed higher and yields moved lower, creating a more risk-on backdrop that pressured the pair to the downside.
